summary.intent: Populate the subSessionId field on open items in the database when a new Claude session is spawned via AppleScript terminal launch.
summary.outcome: subSessionId is now automatically set by reading a GOSSAMER_SPAWN_OPEN_ITEMS environment variable in the SessionStart hook handler, which links spawned sessions back to their originating open items.

repo: The spawn endpoint uses AppleScript fire-and-forget to open a terminal, making synchronous session ID capture impossible.
Claude Code passes its environment variables to hook subprocesses, enabling env-var-based coordination between the spawner and the spawned session.
SessionStart hook in handler.ts is the canonical place to perform DB updates tied to session initialization.

code.finding: Terminal command is now prefixed with GOSSAMER_SPAWN_OPEN_ITEMS='1,2,3' when openItemIds are provided, embedding open item context into the spawned process environment.
On SessionStart, reads GOSSAMER_SPAWN_OPEN_ITEMS from process.env, parses comma-separated IDs, and sets subSessionId = session_id on those open items in the DB.

workflow: When a subprocess cannot return values synchronously (fire-and-forget), passing context via environment variables and reading them in lifecycle hooks is a clean coordination pattern.
Building after edits (tsc) to verify no type errors is a reliable quick check before testing.

friction: AppleScript terminal spawn is inherently fire-and-forget with no mechanism to return the resulting session ID synchronously, requiring an indirect solution.
